phase1.name: generate command
phase1.description: All --format, --generator, --output, --input, --config flags and multi-input / config-precedence scenarios

phase2.name: validation commands + integrations
phase2.description: check, diff, doctor subcommands; cloud providers (vercel/cloudflare/aws); plugins; watch mode; --output-file

findings.title: Error message references unsupported .ts config extension
findings.description: The 'No input file specified' error message tells users to set input in env-typegen.config.ts, but .ts config files cannot be loaded at runtime.
findings.suggestedFix: Change '.config.ts' to '.config.mjs' in the no-input error message string.
findings.impactOnUsers: Users who follow the error hint create an unusable config file.

findings.title: .ts config file in auto-discovery list but immediately errors when found
findings.description: CONFIG_FILE_NAMES includes env-typegen.config.ts. When found, it triggers a hard error instead of gracefully skipping to the next candidate.
findings.suggestedFix: Remove env-typegen.config.ts from CONFIG_FILE_NAMES, or emit a warning and continue to next candidate rather than hard-erroring.
findings.impactOnUsers: TypeScript developers who naturally create a .ts config get a confusing error.

findings.title: Config auto-discovery is silent — no log when a config file is applied
findings.description: When env-typegen.config.mjs is auto-discovered in the CWD, its settings override defaults with no visible indication to the user.
findings.suggestedFix: Log 'ℹ Using config: ./env-typegen.config.mjs' when a config is auto-discovered.
findings.impactOnUsers: Users running from a project root may not understand why they see unexpected output.

findings.title: Multi-input --output basename is silently ignored
findings.description: When using multiple -i inputs, the --output basename is silently dropped; output files are named from input stems. This is undocumented in --help.
findings.suggestedFix: Either document in --help that multi-input ignores --output basename, or introduce --output-dir for multi-input and make --output exclusive to single-input.
findings.impactOnUsers: Users expect -o myoutput.ts to produce myoutput.ts regardless of input count.

findings.title: .env.example in default diff/doctor targets causes ENV_INVALID_TYPE for placeholder values
findings.description: diff and doctor include .env.example in their default --targets. When .env.example has empty placeholder values for URL-typed variables (e.g. DATABASE_READONLY_URL=), the tool raises ENV_INVALID_TYPE. This is incorrect because .env.example is a contract template, not a runtime env file.
findings.suggestedFix: Remove .env.example from the default --targets list for diff and doctor. Keep .env and .env.production as defaults. Document the change in --help.
findings.impactOnUsers: Running diff or doctor out-of-the-box on any project with an .env.example produces phantom errors that confuse users.

findings.title: Doctor command emits per-variable errors for missing default target files
findings.description: When doctor's default targets (.env, .env.production) are absent, the tool emits one ENV_MISSING error per variable per missing file — 39 errors for a 20-variable project. The 'treating as empty' warning is helpful but buried under the error flood.
findings.suggestedFix: Group issues by target file. If a file was not found, emit a single summary line (e.g., '.env — 19 variables missing') instead of individual ENV_MISSING errors.
findings.impactOnUsers: New users running doctor for the first time see an overwhelming wall of errors instead of a clear diagnostic.

recommendations.action: Fix error message: change .config.ts to .config.mjs in the no-input error string
Remove env-typegen.config.ts from CONFIG_FILE_NAMES in src/config.ts
Document multi-input --output basename behavior in --help text
Add a log line when a config file is auto-discovered
Group ENV_MISSING errors for missing target files into a single summary line

overallAssessment.summary: The tool remains functionally correct across all tested scenarios, and the 6 QA findings have now been resolved in source code with regression tests covering the validation target defaults and doctor missing-target behavior.
